description_long: This is a two-story residential house with a gable roof covered in reddish-brown shingles and two brick chimneys. The exterior walls are finished with a combination of gray plaster and a lower section clad in horizontally stacked stonework. The windows are rectangular, framed in brown, and fitted with wooden shutters, some of which are open. The front entry features a small overhang supported by brackets, providing shelter above a single door. The house has a simple, functional design with modest decorative elements, suitable for suburban or urban neighborhoods.
description: A two-story house with a gable roof, stone and plaster exterior, brown-framed windows with shutters, and a sheltered front entry.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, overhang, and symmetrical window arrangement with shutters.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stone and plaster wall, two windows with shutters, and the full expanse of the gable roof.
description_view_2: Rear view with three windows, all with shutters, and the gable roof with two chimneys visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a large window, a small window, and the main entry with overhang.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with rectangular windows and shutters
attribute_entryway_design: Single door with a small overhanging roof, supported by brackets, and stone steps
